message:
fix(http): add Cx cancellation checkpoint to H1 server poll_fn loops

Insert Cx::current().checkpoint() checks at the top of every poll_fn
closure in the HTTP/1.1 connection handler. Previously, if a
connection's Cx region was cancelled (e.g. during graceful shutdown
or task cancellation), the poll_fn loops for reading requests and
flushing responses would continue polling indefinitely, ignoring the
cancellation signal.

Now each poll_fn checks checkpoint().is_err() before doing work:
- Read loop (keep-alive path): returns ReadOutcome::Shutdown
- Read loop (non-keep-alive path): returns ReadOutcome::Shutdown
- Expect 417 rejection flush: returns Interrupted IO error
- Expect 100-continue flush: returns Interrupted IO error
- Response flush: returns Interrupted IO error

This ensures that cancelled connections are torn down promptly rather
than lingering until TCP timeout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
